question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a connection pool exhaustion by sending a high volume of concurrent requests that each hold a HikariDataSource connection, given that the pool is configured via @ConfigurationProperties('spring.datasource.hikari') with no enforced maximum pool size floor, causing a full denial-of-service for all database-dependent endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConditionalOnMissingBean(DataSource.class) condition by supplying a crafted bean definition through an externally reachable configuration path, replacing the legitimate HikariDataSource with a malicious DataSource implementation that intercepts all database credentials (jdbcUrl, username, password)?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the @Lazy-initialized DataSource bean to be triggered at an unexpected time by crafting a request that forces first-use initialization during a high-load window, resulting in a race condition where multiple threads simultaneously call databaseWaiter.waitForDatabase() with the same credentials, leaking connection timing information?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the jdbcUrl, username, or password values indirectly through error messages thrown by databaseWaiter.waitForDatabase() when the database is unreachable, given that DatabaseWaiter logs e.getMessage() which may embed connection string fragments?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the @ConfigurationProperties('spring.datasource.hikari') binding on hikariConfig() to inject arbitrary HikariCP properties (e.g., connectionInitSql, dataSourceClassName) via environment variable or property file manipulation if the deployment environment allows user-controlled configuration, leading to SQL execution at pool initialization?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set spring.datasource.hikari.maximumPoolSize to 1 via an externally writable configuration source, causing the entire application to serialize all database operations through a single connection and producing a denial-of-service for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set spring.datasource.hikari.connectionTimeout to an extremely large value via externally reachable configuration, causing all threads waiting for a connection to block indefinitely and exhausting the application thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious spring.datasource.hikari.connectionInitSql value through an externally writable property source, causing arbitrary SQL to execute against the database under the application's credentials every time a new connection is opened?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hikariConfig.copyStateTo(config) copies all HikariCP state including any injected connectionInitSql or dataSourceProperties into the final HikariDataSource, meaning a poisoned hikariConfig bean leads to persistent SQL injection on every new connection?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the IllegalStateException thrown when the database does not become available within the timeout, causing the application to expose the full JDBC URL (including host, port, and database name) in the exception message that may be surfaced in HTTP error responses?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GENERATE_STATISTICS is set to true in the Hibernate properties, causing Hibernate statistics (query counts, cache hit/miss ratios, entity load counts) to be exposed via an actuator or management endpoint accessible without authentication, leaking internal query patterns?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user leverage the hibernate.criteria.literal_handling_mode=BIND setting being absent from user-controlled query paths to verify whether Criteria API queries actually use bind parameters everywhere, and if any code path bypasses this customizer, inject SQL literals through crafted entity field values?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the CustomJsonFormatMapper set as hibernate.type.json_format_mapper to trigger unsafe Jackson deserialization by submitting a crafted JSON payload in a JSONB column update, given that the underlying OBJECT_MAPPER configuration in ObjectToStringSerializer may not have polymorphic type handling disabled?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HBM2DDL_AUTO=none setting being enforced only at the Hibernate level, while a separate Flyway or Liquibase migration path remains open, to infer schema structure from error messages when submitting malformed data that triggers Hibernate schema validation failures?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpelHelper bean registered under the name 'spelHelper' being accessible in SpEL expressions evaluated against user-supplied input, by crafting a SpEL expression that calls spelHelper.hashCode(byte[]) with attacker-controlled byte arrays to probe internal memory layout or cause hash collision denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the SpelHelper.isNullOrEmpty(Collection<?>) method exposed as a SpEL bean reference to bypass null/empty checks in cache key generation or query filtering, by passing a specially crafted Collection implementation that returns false for isEmpty() but behaves as empty, causing incorrect query results to be cached and served to other users?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a denial-of-service by supplying an extremely large byte[] or Object[] to SpelHelper.hashCode() through a SpEL-evaluated cache key expression, causing excessive CPU consumption during Arrays.hashCode() computation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SpelHelper is registered for reflection in CommonRuntimeHints (METHODS_ONLY) in the native image, allowing reflective invocation of all SpelHelper methods at runtime, potentially bypassing access controls that rely on proxy-based interception which is absent due to proxyBeanMethods=false?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the hiero.mirror.common.shard or hiero.mirror.common.realm configuration properties (if externally writable) to alter the EntityId values computed in SystemEntity (e.g., addressBookFile101, treasuryAccount), causing the mirror node to misidentify system entities and process attacker-controlled data as authoritative system file updates?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.common.realm or hiero.mirror.common.shard to Long.MAX_VALUE via an externally reachable configuration path, causing EntityId.of() to overflow and produce a negative or zero entity ID that collides with a legitimate system entity, bypassing entity-type checks downstream?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of SystemEntity fields (e.g., addressBookFile101, feeCollectionAccount) in a multi-threaded environment to observe a partially constructed EntityId through a data race, given that Lombok's @Getter(lazy=true) uses double-checked locking that may be unsafe under certain JVM memory models?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase() retry loop to run indefinitely by manipulating the hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.timeout property to Duration.MAX_VALUE via an externally writable configuration source, blocking application startup and causing a permanent denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that DatabaseWaiter calls DriverManager.getConnection() directly with the raw jdbcUrl, allowing a JDBC URL injection attack where a crafted jdbcUrl containing additional parameters (e.g., allowLoadLocalInfile=true) enables data exfiltration if the URL is partially user-influenced?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated failed connection attempts in DatabaseWaiter.waitForDatabase() by making the database temporarily unreachable, causing the application to log the JDBC connection error message (including partial credentials or host information) repeatedly, enabling information harvesting from log aggregation systems?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.common.databaseStartup.interval to 500ms (minimum allowed) via externally writable config, causing the DatabaseWaiter retry loop to hammer the database with connection attempts at maximum rate, producing a denial-of-service against the database server?
